original_sources.evidence: checkDeathGaze queries an inflated range AABB, requires Entity.canBeCollidedWith(), then calls EntityUtils.isVisibleTo(0.75F, player, entity, range), line of sight, PVP admission and absence of Wither.
isVisibleTo targets entity x/z and boundingBox.minY + height/2, builds an axis of look*range, and delegates to Utils.isLyingInCone.
isLyingInCone uses halfAperture=aperture/2, cosine comparison against cos(halfAperture), then strict axial projection < axis length as a round cap.

pre_fix_discrepancy: The port compared a normalized dot product directly against 0.75, making the cone almost twice as wide as TC4.
The port aimed at target eye position instead of the original body midpoint.
The port imposed Euclidean distance <= range, while TC4 used the cone axial round-cap projection.
The port did not preserve the original canBeCollidedWith/isPickable admission gate.
production_contract: TC4WarpRuntimeParity.deathGazeConeContains implements full aperture 0.75 radians, cosine(aperture/2), and strict axial projection < range.
WarpEvents.checkDeathGaze targets the living entity body midpoint and delegates geometry to the shared production helper.
WarpEvents uses isPickable before cone/LOS/PVP/effect checks and no longer prefilters by spherical distance.
